AT A GLANCE
Your Trip Overview
Welcome! We've put together a comprehensive look at everything for your family's Disney World trip. This proposal covers flights from Midland, vacation home and resort options, park passes, VIP experiences, character dining, special tours, and more — all priced for your group of 7. Scroll through at your pace and tap anything that interests you.
Travel Dates
May 12 – 17
5 nights, 6 days
Travelers
7 Total
2 parents · 2 grandmas · 3 kids (1 toddler)
Origin
Midland, TX (MAF)
Southwest Airlines
Destination
Orlando, FL (MCO)
Walt Disney World Resort
Lodging

Ground Transport
Rental Vehicle

What's Covered in This Proposal
Southwest flights from Midland (MAF) — 2 itinerary options
3 vacation home options (VRBO/Airbnb) with private pools
Complete Disney Resort info sheet — all 23 properties with descriptions & ratings
Park passes — 5-day options for 7 travelers including toddler

Special park tours, fireworks cruises, after-hours events
Rental vehicle options — minivan & large SUV
Next: Flight Options
FLIGHTS
Midland to Orlando
Southwest Airlines flies from Midland (MAF) to Orlando (MCO) with one connection — typically through Dallas Love Field (DAL) or Houston Hobby (HOU). There are no nonstop options from Midland. Here are the two itineraries we've identified so far. All prices include bags (Southwest includes 2 free checked bags per person). Tap the one you prefer.
OPTION A · MORNING DEPARTURE
6:50 AM MAF → Connect → 12:35 PM MCO
1 stop (likely DAL or HOU) · Arrive by lunch · ~5h 45m total
~$2,700
OPTION B · AFTERNOON DEPARTURE
2:45 PM MAF → Connect → 8:25 PM MCO
1 stop (likely DAL or HOU) · Evening arrival · ~5h 40m total
~$2,500
Agent's Note: Prices are estimated for all 7 travelers (~$2,500 starting). Southwest includes 2 free checked bags per person and has no change fees. The morning option gets you to Orlando by lunch — giving you time to pick up the rental car, get to your lodging, and possibly hit Disney Springs that evening. The afternoon option saves a couple hundred dollars but means your first real park day is May 13. We'll pull exact flight numbers and connection cities once you choose a preference. Children under 2 may fly free on a lap, but for safety and comfort with a toddler, purchasing a seat is recommended.
Next: Vacation Home Options
LODGING TRACK A
Vacation Home Rental
For a family of 7 with a toddler, a vacation home gives you the most space, a private pool, a kitchen for easy meals, and a washer/dryer for inevitable kid messes. We recommend the Windsor Hills neighborhood — a gated resort community just 2 miles from Disney's main entrance with a clubhouse, water park-style pool, movie theater, game room, and playground. All the homes in the community are well-appointed with private pools, and pricing falls in your $1,100–$2,300 range for 5 nights.
Recommended · Star Wars Game Room & Private Pool
Magical 4-Bedroom Villa
Windsor Hills · Kissimmee, FL · 2 miles from Disney
Bedrooms
4 bedrooms · 4 bathrooms
Size
1,824 sq ft · Sleeps 8
Check-in / Out
May 12, 4pm / May 17, 10am
Rating
10.0 Exceptional · 155 reviews
Private PoolHot TubFree Pool HeatFull KitchenWasher/DryerStar Wars Game RoomArcade CabinetPack & Play AvailableGated Community
STARTING FROM · 5 NIGHTS
☝ TAP HERE TO SELECT THIS OPTION
from$1,732.63
~$347/night · includes taxes
Agent's Note: This villa sleeps 8 comfortably — perfect for your group of 7. Everyone gets privacy with 4 separate bedrooms and bathrooms. The private heated pool is a lifesaver after long park days, and the Star Wars game room with arcade cabinet will keep the kids entertained on your rest day. Free cancellation through April 28, 50% penalty through May 5, then non-refundable.
Why Windsor Hills? This gated resort community sits just 2 miles from Walt Disney World's main entrance. The neighborhood clubhouse features a water park-style pool with slides, a lazy river, a screening room for movie nights, a fitness center, game room, and playground — all included at no extra charge for guests. Every home in the community has a private pool. We can source additional homes here if this one books up — pricing in the community typically runs $1,100–$2,300 for 5 nights depending on size and season.
Next: Disney Resort Info Sheet
LODGING TRACK B
Disney Resort Hotels — Complete Guide
Here's every Disney resort option with descriptions, our experience ratings, and proximity to parks. Rates shown are per room, per night (before tax). For your family of 7, you'll likely need 2 rooms at most resorts (some suites/villas sleep larger groups). We've estimated 2-room totals for 5 nights. Resort guests get 30-minute early park entry, free Disney transportation, and MagicBand+ integration — huge perks with kids.
Note on Pricing: Rates were captured from Disney's site showing 5/13–5/17 for 2 Adults + 2 Children. Your actual party (5 adults counting grandmas + 2 older kids + toddler across 2 rooms, 5/12–5/17) will differ. These are directional — we'll confirm exact quotes at booking. Mouse ear ratings (🏰) are our assessment of overall experience quality on a 1–5 scale.
Value Resorts · Budget-Friendly with Big Disney Theming
The Campsites at Fort Wilderness Resort
Magic Kingdom Resort Area · Campgrounds · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$136.50/nt
~$1,365 est. / 2 sites × 5 nights
Opened in 1971, Fort Wilderness is one of Disney's original properties — a 750-acre woodland campground that feels worlds away from the parks. Campsite guests bring their own RV or tent. The resort features nightly campfire sing-alongs with Chip & Dale, horseback riding, and the beloved Hoop-Dee-Doo Musical Revue dinner show. Boat service to Magic Kingdom is a hidden gem.
📍 Boat ride to Magic Kingdom (~10 min) · Bus to all other parks
RV/Tent OnlyBoat to MKCampfire ProgramsMost Unique Setting
Disney's All-Star Music Resort
Animal Kingdom Area · Value · Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰
$196.50/nt
~$1,965 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
One of three All-Star resorts opened in 1994, Music features oversized musical instruments and genre-themed buildings (jazz, rock, country, Broadway). Rooms are compact but recently refurbished. The family suites here sleep up to 6 and include a kitchenette — potentially allowing your group to book just 2 suites instead of 3 standard rooms.
📍 Bus only to all parks (~15–20 min rides) · Farthest resort area from MK
Family Suites AvailableFood CourtRecently RefurbishedBudget Pick
Disney's All-Star Movies Resort
Animal Kingdom Area · Value · Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰
$228.00/nt
~$2,280 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
The most kid-friendly of the three All-Star resorts, opened in 1999. Buildings are themed around beloved Disney films — Toy Story, 101 Dalmatians, Fantasia, The Mighty Ducks, and The Love Bug. Giant character statues throughout the property make kids feel like they've shrunk into a movie set. Standard rooms only (no suites), refurbished in recent years.
📍 Bus only to all parks (~15–20 min) · Same area as other All-Stars
Most Kid-Friendly ThemingFood CourtRefurbished Rooms
Disney's Pop Century Resort
Wide World of Sports Area · Value · Bus, Skyliner, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$250.75/nt
~$2,508 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened in 2003 and themed around pop culture icons of the 1950s–1990s — think giant Rubik's Cubes, yo-yos, and bowling pins. The big advantage here is Skyliner access, Disney's gondola system that whisks you to EPCOT and Hollywood Studios in minutes. Rooms were fully refurbished with queen beds, fold-down beds, and updated bathrooms. One of the most popular Value resorts.
📍 Skyliner to EPCOT (~15 min) & Hollywood Studios (~5 min) · Bus to MK & AK
Skyliner AccessRefurbishedBest Value for LocationFood Court
Disney's Art of Animation Resort
Wide World of Sports Area · Value · Bus, Skyliner, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$305.25/nt
~$3,053 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened in 2012, this is Disney's newest Value resort and widely considered the best. Stunning animation-themed buildings celebrate The Little Mermaid, Cars, Finding Nemo, and The Lion King. The family suites (Nemo and Cars themed) sleep up to 6 with separate living areas and kitchenettes — ideal for families with young kids. The Big Blue Pool is the largest at any Disney resort. Shares a Skyliner station with Pop Century.
📍 Skyliner to EPCOT & Hollywood Studios · Bus to MK & AK
Family SuitesSkylinerBest Value ResortLargest Disney PoolGreat for Kids
Moderate Resorts · More Space, Better Dining, Themed Pools
Disney's Port Orleans — French Quarter
Disney Springs Area · Moderate · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$328.25/nt
~$3,283 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
The smallest and most walkable Moderate resort, opened 1991. Themed after the French Quarter of New Orleans with wrought-iron balconies, cobblestone streets, and Mardi Gras flair. The Doubloon Lagoon pool features a sea serpent water slide. Recently renovated rooms are among the best at this tier. Boat service to Disney Springs is lovely, and you can easily walk to sister property Riverside.
📍 Boat to Disney Springs (~15 min) · Bus to all 4 parks (~15–20 min)
Smallest/Most WalkableNew RoomsBoat to Disney SpringsGreat Theming
Disney's Caribbean Beach Resort
EPCOT Area · Moderate · Bus, Skyliner, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$334.75/nt
~$3,348 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened in 1988, this sprawling tropical resort features "villages" themed after Caribbean islands surrounding a 45-acre lake. The major selling point is Skyliner access — the gondola station is at the resort's hub. Old Port Royale serves as the central dining and shopping area. Some rooms have been updated with pirate theming that kids love. The resort is large, so room location matters.
📍 Skyliner hub to EPCOT & Hollywood Studios · Bus to MK & AK
Skyliner HubPirate Rooms Available45-Acre LakeTable Service Dining
Disney's Port Orleans — Riverside
Disney Springs Area · Moderate · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$365.00/nt
~$3,650 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 1992 and widely considered the most beautiful Moderate resort. Themed after the antebellum South with two distinct areas: stately Magnolia Bend mansions and rustic Alligator Bayou cottages tucked among cypress trees. The Ol' Man Island pool has a waterfall and slide. Royal Guest Rooms with princess-themed headboards are available. Gorgeous grounds are perfect for evening strolls. Shares boat service to Disney Springs with French Quarter.
📍 Boat to Disney Springs · Bus to all 4 parks (~15–20 min)
Most Beautiful ModerateRoyal Guest RoomsNew RoomsTable ServiceBoat to Springs
Disney's Coronado Springs Resort
Animal Kingdom Area · Moderate · Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$409.00/nt
~$4,090 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Originally opened 1997, transformed by the 2019 addition of Gran Destino Tower — a stunning 15-story lakeside tower with rooms that rival Deluxe quality. The resort is themed after Spanish-colonial Mexico and the American Southwest. Gran Destino Tower rooms feature modern décor, excellent views, and a rooftop restaurant (Toledo). The older casitas are more budget-friendly. This doubles as Disney's primary convention hotel, so it has more of an adult/upscale vibe.
📍 Bus to all parks (~15–25 min) · No boat or Skyliner access
Gran Destino TowerBest Moderate RoomsRooftop DiningConvention Hotel Vibe
Deluxe Resort Hotels · Premium Location, Dining & Transportation
Disney's Animal Kingdom Lodge
Animal Kingdom Area · Deluxe · Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰🏰
$565.75/nt
~$5,658 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 2001 and arguably Disney's most immersive resort experience. Built around a 33-acre savanna where over 200 animals — giraffes, zebras, ostriches — roam freely, visible from most room balconies. The lobby is a soaring, six-story African lodge inspired by real game lodges. Features award-winning restaurants Jiko and Boma, a small museum of African artifacts, campfire storytelling, and cultural experiences. The only downside is its remote location — bus only to all parks.
📍 Bus only to all parks (~15–25 min) · Most remote Deluxe location
Live Animal SavannaMost Immersive ResortAward-Winning DiningCultural ProgramsBest Deluxe Value
Disney's Wilderness Lodge
Magic Kingdom Area · Deluxe · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$624.25/nt
~$6,243 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 1994, inspired by the grand national park lodges of the American Pacific Northwest — especially Old Faithful Inn. The towering timber lobby features an 82-foot fireplace, totem poles, and a spring that flows through the lobby and out to the pool (the "fire rock geyser" erupts regularly). Whispering Canyon Café is a rowdy, family-friendly character dining spot. Boat service to Magic Kingdom is magical at night. Story Book Dining at Artist Point offers an intimate Snow White dinner experience.
📍 Boat to Magic Kingdom (~10 min) · Bus to EPCOT, HS & AK
Boat to MKNational Park ThemingCharacter DiningGeyser Pool
Disney's Beach Club Resort
EPCOT Area · Deluxe · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰🏰
$719.25/nt
~$7,193 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 1990, themed as a New England seaside resort with pastel colors and beachfront ambience. The crown jewel is Stormalong Bay — a 3-acre sand-bottom pool complex shared with Yacht Club featuring a 150-foot waterslide, lazy river, and shipwreck play area. It's widely considered the best pool at any Disney resort and a destination unto itself. Walking distance to both EPCOT (International Gateway entrance) and Hollywood Studios. Boat service around Crescent Lake.
📍 Walk to EPCOT (~5 min) & Hollywood Studios (~15 min) · Boat to both
Best Pool on PropertyWalk to 2 ParksStormalong BayGreat for Families
Disney's Contemporary Resort
Magic Kingdom Area · Deluxe · Monorail, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$1,015.50/nt
~$10,155 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
An original 1971 opening-day resort — the iconic A-frame building where the monorail glides through the lobby. The only resort where you can walk to Magic Kingdom (10-minute path). Chef Mickey's character buffet is here. The top-floor California Grill offers fireworks views and prix fixe dining. Tower rooms have been modernized; Garden Wing rooms are more dated but affordable. The Incredibles-themed pool is popular with kids.
📍 Walk to Magic Kingdom (~10 min) · Monorail to EPCOT · Bus to HS & AK
Walk to MKMonorailChef Mickey'sCalifornia GrillOriginal 1971
Disney's BoardWalk Inn
EPCOT Area · Deluxe · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$1,036.50/nt
~$10,365 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 1996 on Crescent Lake, evoking a 1940s Atlantic City boardwalk. The resort's real draw is the BoardWalk entertainment district — a quarter-mile promenade with restaurants, shops, street performers, and carnival games along the waterfront. Walking distance to EPCOT and Hollywood Studios. Flying Fish restaurant is excellent. The Luna Park pool has a roller-coaster themed slide. Rooms are being refreshed through 2026–2027.
📍 Walk to EPCOT (~10 min) & Hollywood Studios (~15 min) · Boat to both
Walk to 2 ParksBoardWalk EntertainmentFlying FishEvening Atmosphere
Deluxe Villas (DVC) · Kitchens, Extra Space, Home-Away-From-Home
Disney's Old Key West Resort
Disney Springs Area · DVC Villas · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$548.00/nt
~$5,480 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
The original Disney Vacation Club resort, opened 1991. Themed after Key West, Florida with pastel buildings, white picket fences, and a laid-back tropical vibe. Studios, 1-bedroom, and 2-bedroom villas with full kitchens. The most spacious rooms in the DVC portfolio. Sprawling grounds with a nice pool and boat dock. Feels more like a condo community than a resort — which is either a pro or con depending on your style.
📍 Boat to Disney Springs · Bus to all 4 parks (~15–20 min)
Full KitchensMost Spacious RoomsBoat to SpringsOriginal DVC
The Cabins at Fort Wilderness (DVC)
Magic Kingdom Area · DVC Villas · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$568.00/nt
~$5,680 est. / 2 cabins × 5 nights
Brand-new modern cabins that replaced the older cabin-style accommodation at Fort Wilderness. These standalone DVC cabins sleep up to 6 with a bedroom, living area, full kitchen, and covered porch in a private wooded setting. Perfect for families who want the Disney bubble but a bit more independence and nature. Access to all Fort Wilderness activities including the campfire, horse rides, and boat service to Magic Kingdom.
📍 Boat to Magic Kingdom (~10 min) · Bus to all other parks
Standalone CabinsBrand NewFull KitchenWooded SettingBoat to MK
Animal Kingdom Villas — Kidani Village
Animal Kingdom Area · DVC Villas · Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰🏰
$574.50/nt
~$5,745 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 2009 as a DVC expansion to Animal Kingdom Lodge. Has its own savanna viewing areas, the excellent Sanaa restaurant (bread service is legendary), and Samawati Springs pool. Villas include full kitchens and washer/dryers. The 2-bedroom villas sleep 8+ and could potentially house your entire group in a single unit — a huge cost saver. Same immersive African theming and animal viewing as the main Lodge.
📍 Bus only to all parks · Shares facilities with AK Lodge
Savanna ViewsFull Kitchen2BR Fits 8+Sanaa RestaurantWasher/Dryer
Disney's BoardWalk Villas
EPCOT Area · DVC Villas · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$712.50/nt
~$7,125 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
DVC villas sharing the BoardWalk complex. Same fantastic location walking distance to EPCOT and Hollywood Studios. Villas include kitchens/kitchenettes, washer/dryers, and more space than the Inn. Access to all BoardWalk entertainment. The 2-bedroom villas sleep up to 8.
📍 Walk to EPCOT (~10 min) & Hollywood Studios (~15 min)
Walk to 2 ParksFull KitchenBoardWalk LocationWasher/Dryer
Animal Kingdom Villas — Jambo House
Animal Kingdom Area · DVC Villas · Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰🏰
$799.50/nt
~$7,995 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
DVC villas within the main Animal Kingdom Lodge building itself. You're in the iconic lobby with the soaring thatched ceiling and direct savanna views. Fewer villa units than Kidani, making it feel more exclusive. Access to Jiko and Boma restaurants without leaving your building. Premium savanna-view rooms let you watch giraffes from bed.
📍 Bus only to all parks · In the main AKL building
In Main LodgeSavanna ViewsJiko & Boma AccessMost Exclusive AKL
Disney's Riviera Resort
EPCOT Area · DVC Villas · Bus, Skyliner, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰🏰
$1,270.50/nt
~$12,705 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Disney's newest DVC resort, opened 2019, inspired by Walt Disney's travels along the European Riviera. Stunning architecture with grand porticos and an original art collection throughout. Topolino's Terrace rooftop restaurant is one of the best character breakfasts on property and has incredible fireworks views at dinner. Direct Skyliner access to EPCOT and Hollywood Studios. Tower Studios are tiny but affordable; 2-bedroom villas are spectacular.
📍 Skyliner to EPCOT & Hollywood Studios · Bus to MK & AK
Newest DVCSkylinerTopolino's TerraceEuropean EleganceRooftop Dining
Disney's Saratoga Springs Resort & Spa
Disney Springs Area · DVC Villas · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰
$1,392.00/nt
~$13,920 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
Opened 2004 themed after the historic horse-racing resort town of Saratoga Springs, NY. The largest DVC resort with a sprawling layout. Walking distance and boat access to Disney Springs. Features a full spa, multiple pools, and the Treehouse Villas — elevated octagonal treehouses sleeping 9. The Treehouses could potentially house your entire group. Resort feels more spread out and less intimate than other DVCs.
📍 Walk/boat to Disney Springs · Bus to all 4 parks (~15–20 min)
Treehouse VillasFull SpaWalk to Disney SpringsLargest DVC
Disney's Beach Club Villas
EPCOT Area · DVC Villas · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰🏰
$1,616.50/nt
~$16,165 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
DVC villas attached to Beach Club Resort. Full access to Stormalong Bay (the best pool on property), walking distance to two parks, and villa kitchens/kitchenettes. Combines the best location at Disney World with the space and amenities of a villa. 2-bedroom units sleep 8. The premium price reflects the unbeatable combination of pool, parks proximity, and villa space.
📍 Walk to EPCOT (~5 min) & Hollywood Studios (~15 min) · Stormalong Bay
Best Pool + Best LocationWalk to 2 ParksFull KitchenPremium
Boulder Ridge Villas at Wilderness Lodge
Magic Kingdom Area · DVC Villas · Boat, Bus, Taxi
🏰🏰🏰🏰
$1,781.75/nt
~$17,818 est. / 2 rooms × 5 nights
DVC villas within the Wilderness Lodge complex, sharing the stunning Pacific Northwest-themed lobby, geyser pool, and boat service to Magic Kingdom. Rooms feature natural stone and wood elements. Full kitchens and washer/dryers in 1BR+ units. Access to all Wilderness Lodge dining and amenities including Story Book Dining at Artist Point and Whispering Canyon Café.
📍 Boat to Magic Kingdom (~10 min) · Bus to other parks
Boat to MKWilderness Lodge AccessFull KitchenGeyser Pool
Agent's Note — Family of 7 Strategy: For your group, the DVC 2-bedroom villas are worth a close look — many sleep 8–9 guests, meaning you might only need one unit instead of two standard rooms. Art of Animation family suites (sleep 6) are the budget-friendly equivalent — you'd need 2 suites but they include kitchenettes. The Saratoga Springs Treehouse Villas sleep 9 in a single unit. If a resort interests you, tell us and we'll pull the exact quote for your dates and party configuration.
Next: Park Tickets
PARK ADMISSION
Tickets & Passes
Park tickets for 7 travelers — that's 4 adults (ages 10+), 2 children (ages 3–9), and 1 toddler. Disney charges for ages 3+ so the toddler's ticket depends on their exact age. If under 3, they're free. We're pricing all 7 to be safe as you requested. Mid-May is moderate demand. Tap the option you prefer.
5-Day Base Ticket (1 park per day)
~$540–$680/person (adult) · ~$510–$645/person (child 3–9)
Est. for 7 travelers: $3,750–$4,700 + 6.5% tax
Visit one park each day. Best per-day value. You can visit any of the 4 parks but only one per day. Great if you plan one dedicated day per park.
5-Day Park Hopper Ticket
~$740–$880/person (adult) · ~$710–$845/person (child)
Est. for 7 travelers: $5,050–$6,100 + 6.5% tax
Visit multiple parks in one day. Start at Animal Kingdom in the morning, hop to Magic Kingdom for fireworks. Maximum flexibility — especially useful with VIP Tours that cross parks.
4-Day Base Ticket (rest day built in)
~$510–$660/person (adult) · ~$485–$630/person (child)
Est. for 7 travelers: $3,500–$4,500 + 6.5% tax
Smart if you want a pool/rest day mid-trip. With 3 kids including a toddler, a down day at the vacation home pool might be exactly what everyone needs.
Agent's Note: Disney uses date-based pricing, so exact costs depend on your start date. Children 3–9 get a slight discount (~$5/day less). If the toddler is under 3 at time of travel, they're free — saving $500+. We strongly recommend Park Hopper if you're doing a VIP Tour, as the guide will want to hop between parks.
Next: VIP Access
SKIP THE LINES
VIP Tours & Premier Access
This is the big one — unlimited line skipping and VIP park access. Two main options, very different in cost and experience. All pricing below is calculated for your group of 7. Tap any that interest you.
Disney Private VIP Tour — Full Day
$450–$950/hour · 7-hour minimum · Up to 10 guests
Est. for 7 travelers (8-hr tour): $3,600–$7,600 per day (flat rate, not per person)
The ultimate Disney experience. A dedicated "plaid vest" guide plans your entire day, escorts your group through every Lightning Lane with unlimited access and re-rides, provides backstage transportation between parks via private vehicle, reserved fireworks/parade viewing, and complimentary snacks and water all day. Your guide handles strollers, holds bags, and knows every shortcut. For a family with a toddler and grandparents, this is game-changing — no waiting, no stress, no phone app management. Park tickets with Park Hopper are required separately. Book at (407) 560-4033 up to 60 days in advance.
Lightning Lane Premier Pass — Per Day
$129–$449 per person, per park, per day
Est. for 7 travelers per day: $900–$3,150 (mid-May ~$150–$350/person)
Self-guided skip-the-line. One-time entry to every Lightning Lane in one park, no scheduling needed. However: one ride per attraction, one park per day only, no guide, no backstage transport, no reserved viewing. You manage it all via the app. For 7 people over 4–5 park days, this adds up to $3,600–$15,750. Can sell out in advance for popular parks.
Lightning Lane Multi Pass — Budget Option
$15–$39 per person, per day
Est. for 7 travelers per day: $105–$273
The standard skip-the-line service (replaces old Genie+). Covers most Lightning Lane rides but NOT the top-tier attractions (those require separate Single Pass purchases at $10–$25 each). You book return windows via the app and can hold 3 at a time. The most affordable option but requires the most phone management. Good for lighter park days.
Agent's Note: My recommendation for your group — the VIP Tour is worth it for at least 1–2 of your biggest park days. With 3 kids (one in a stroller), 2 grandmas who may tire faster, and the Florida May heat, having a guide who manages every logistics detail is invaluable. The guide stays with the stroller while the thrill-seekers ride, keeps everyone hydrated, and maximizes your time. For your other park days, Multi Pass keeps costs reasonable. We can design a mix-and-match strategy.
Next: Character Dining
CHARACTER EXPERIENCES
Character Dining & Special Meals
Characters come right to your table — no waiting in meet-and-greet lines. All prices below are calculated for your full group of 7 (4 adults + 2 children 3–9 + 1 toddler priced as child). Reservations open 60 days in advance. Tap any meals that interest you and we'll prioritize those reservations.
IN-PARK CHARACTER DINING (Park Ticket Required)
Cinderella's Royal Table — Magic Kingdom
~$88/adult · ~$52/child
Est. for 7: ~$508 + gratuity
Dine inside Cinderella Castle with princess table visits. The most iconic character meal at Disney. Extremely hard to book — must reserve at exactly 60 days. The kids will remember this one forever.
Crystal Palace — Magic Kingdom
~$62/adult · ~$42/child
Est. for 7: ~$374 + gratuity
Winnie the Pooh, Tigger, Eeyore, and Piglet. Victorian glasshouse buffet on Main Street. Very toddler-friendly — Pooh characters are gentle and approachable for little ones.
Tusker House — Animal Kingdom
~$52–$64/adult · ~$33–$42/child
Est. for 7: ~$307–$388 + gratuity
Donald, Mickey, Goofy, and Daisy in safari outfits. Buffet with American and African-inspired dishes. Breakfast is the best value. Great energy — kids love the safari costumes.
Hollywood & Vine — Hollywood Studios
~$49–$64/adult · ~$33–$44/child
Est. for 7: ~$295–$388 + gratuity
Minnie's Seasonal Dine — Mickey, Minnie, Goofy, and Pluto in seasonal costumes. Buffet. Breakfast is the cheapest character meal option here. Fun seasonal outfits the kids won't see anywhere else.
Akershus Royal Banquet Hall — EPCOT (Norway)
~$62–$69/adult · ~$39–$46/child
Est. for 7: ~$365–$414 + gratuity
Disney princess dining in a medieval Norwegian castle. Meet Belle, Aurora, Snow White, Ariel, and more. Family-style meal. An excellent princess experience that's easier to book than Cinderella's Royal Table.
RESORT CHARACTER DINING (No Park Ticket Needed)
Chef Mickey's — Contemporary Resort
~$69/adult · ~$44/child
Est. for 7: ~$408 + gratuity
The classic Fab Five buffet — Mickey, Minnie, Donald, Goofy, Pluto all in chef attire. High energy, great for all ages. No park ticket needed. Take the monorail — kids love it. Perfect for arrival or departure day.
'Ohana Breakfast — Polynesian Village
~$53/adult · ~$33/child
Est. for 7: ~$311 + gratuity
Lilo, Stitch, Mickey, and Pluto in Hawaiian outfits. Family-style breakfast with Mickey waffles, pineapple bread, and loaded skillets. One of the most beloved character meals at Disney. Very popular — book early. No park ticket needed.
Topolino's Terrace — Riviera Resort
~$54/adult · ~$33/child
Est. for 7: ~$315 + gratuity
Mickey, Minnie, Donald, Daisy in Riviera artist outfits. Upscale breakfast with rooftop views. More intimate and elegant than typical character meals — grandmas will love the ambiance. No park ticket needed.
1900 Park Fare — Grand Floridian
~$58–$69/adult · ~$37–$44/child
Est. for 7: ~$343–$408 + gratuity
Breakfast with characters like Mary Poppins. Dinner features Cinderella and friends. Elegant Grand Floridian setting — feels special. No park ticket needed.
Story Book Dining at Artist Point — Wilderness Lodge
~$69/adult · ~$43/child
Est. for 7: ~$405 + gratuity
Snow White themed prix-fixe dinner in an enchanted forest. Intimate and quieter — 3 courses. Dinner only. A more special-occasion feel than the buffet options. No park ticket needed.
Agent's Note: I'd recommend 2–3 character meals across the trip. For your arrival day (no park ticket yet), 'Ohana or Chef Mickey's are perfect. For an in-park day, Crystal Palace is the most toddler-friendly. For a "wow" moment, Cinderella's Royal Table is unforgettable. All prices are before 18–20% gratuity. Budget roughly $350–$600 per character meal for your group of 7 after tip.
Next: Special Experiences
ENCHANTING EXTRAS
Special Tours, Fireworks & Experiences
Beyond rides and dining — these are the experiences that create once-in-a-lifetime memories. All priced for your group of 7 where applicable. Tap any that interest you.
FIREWORKS & CRUISES
Fireworks Pontoon Cruise — Seven Seas Lagoon
~$399–$449 per boat (up to 10 guests)
Est. for 7: ~$399–$449 total (one boat fits everyone!)
Watch Magic Kingdom fireworks from the water on a private pontoon. No park ticket needed. Includes snacks and beverages. The toddler can move freely on the boat, grandmas can sit comfortably, and you get an unobstructed view. One of the most relaxed and memorable experiences at Disney — and at per-boat pricing, it's actually a great deal for larger families.
Fireworks Dessert Party — Magic Kingdom
~$99–$139 per person
Est. for 7: ~$693–$973 + tax
Reserved viewing for Happily Ever After fireworks plus a spread of desserts and specialty drinks. Stress-free — no staking out a spot 2 hours early. Park admission required. The premium price per person makes the pontoon cruise a better value for your group size.
AFTER HOURS EVENTS (Separate Ticket — No Day Pass Needed)
Disney After Hours — Magic Kingdom
$175–$199 per person + tax
Est. for 7: ~$1,225–$1,393 + tax
10pm–1am with near walk-on waits for all major rides. Includes unlimited ice cream, popcorn, and beverages. No separate day ticket needed. Select nights through July 2026. Note: this is a late night event — may not be ideal with a toddler and grandparents unless the little one is a night owl.
Disney After Hours — Hollywood Studios
$155–$189 per person + tax
Est. for 7: ~$1,085–$1,323 + tax
Walk onto Rise of the Resistance, Slinky Dog Dash, Tower of Terror, and more. Same unlimited snacks deal. Select nights through September 2026.
BEHIND-THE-SCENES TOURS
Keys to the Kingdom Tour — Magic Kingdom
~$169–$189 per person
Ages 16+ only — Est. for 4 eligible adults: ~$676–$756
The legendary 5-hour backstage tour including the Utilidor tunnels beneath Magic Kingdom. Includes lunch and keepsake pin. Park admission required. Adults only (16+) — the kids would need separate supervision during this tour. A bucket-list experience for any Disney fan.
KID-FOCUSED EXPERIENCES
Bibbidi Bobbidi Boutique — Magic Kingdom
~$100–$450+ per child (varies by package)
Est. per child: $100–$450 depending on package chosen
Royal makeover for kids ages 3–12. Fairy Godmother apprentices style hair, makeup, nails, and costume. Packages range from basic hair styling to full princess/knight transformation with costume, accessories, and photo package. A magical experience before dining at Cinderella's Royal Table.
Savi's Workshop — Lightsaber Building (Hollywood Studios)
~$249.99 per lightsaber
Per builder (1 guest may accompany). Park admission required.
Build a custom lightsaber in Star Wars: Galaxy's Edge. Immersive 20-minute ceremony. One builder per reservation, one companion can watch. A must for any Star Wars fan in the family. The finished lightsaber is a premium souvenir.
Capture Your Moment — Professional PhotoPass
~$50 per 20-minute session
Est. for 7 (1 session): $50 total
A dedicated Disney photographer for a 20-minute session at an iconic location (Cinderella Castle, Tree of Life, etc.). All 7 of you in front of the castle — perfect family photo. Book in advance. Park admission required.
Agent's Note: My top picks for your family: the fireworks cruise (best value for 7 people and toddler-friendly), Bibbidi Bobbidi Boutique if any of the kids want a princess/knight transformation, and the Capture Your Moment photo session for a professional family shot in front of the castle. If the adults want a special experience, Keys to the Kingdom is unforgettable — grandmas can watch the kids at the pool or hotel during the tour.
Next: Rental Vehicles
GROUND TRANSPORTATION
Rental Vehicles
For 7 travelers (4 adults, 3 kids including a car seat/booster situation), you'll want something spacious. Both options below seat 7–8 comfortably with luggage room. Tap your preference.
Minivan — Chrysler Pacifica or Toyota Sienna
Starting from ~$35–$60/day
Est. 5 days: ~$175–$300
Seats 7–8. Sliding doors make car seat loading easy in tight Disney parking structures. Stow-and-go seating for extra cargo. The most practical choice for a family with young kids. Bring your own car seats or rent from the agency (~$15/day each).
Large SUV — Chevy Tahoe / Suburban / Ford Expedition
Starting from ~$50–$90/day
Est. 5 days: ~$250–$450
Seats 7–8 with commanding road presence and maximum cargo room. The Suburban is the roomiest option for 7 + stroller + luggage. Third row is tighter than a minivan for adults. The premium comfort/style choice.
Agent's Note: For 7 people with a stroller, park bags, and Florida sun gear, the minivan's sliding doors and flat-fold seating are hard to beat practically. Budget ~$15–20/day for Disney theme park parking if staying off-property. Resort guests get free parking at the parks.
Next: Cost Summary
COST SUMMARY
Estimated Trip Investment
Here's a planning framework showing what different combinations would run for your family of 7. These are ranges — the final number depends on which options you choose. Let us know your preferences and we'll dial in exact figures.
ItemEst. Range (7 Travelers)
Southwest Flights (MAF → MCO, 7 travelers)$2,500 – $2,700
Lodging — VRBO Villa (5 nights)$1,100 – $2,300
Lodging — Disney Resort (2 rooms × 5 nights)$1,365 – $17,818
Park Tickets — 5-Day Base (7 people + tax)$4,000 – $5,000
Park Tickets — 5-Day Hopper (7 people + tax)$5,400 – $6,500
VIP Tour (1 day, 8 hours — flat rate)$3,600 – $7,600
LL Premier Pass (per day, 7 people)$900 – $3,150/day
LL Multi Pass (per day, 7 people)$105 – $273/day
Character Dining (per meal, 7 people + tip)$350 – $600/meal
Fireworks Cruise (1 boat, all 7 fit)$399 – $449
Rental Minivan (5 days)$175 – $300
Rental Large SUV (5 days)$250 – $450
Theme Park Parking (per day, off-property)$30/day · ~$150 total
Budget Snapshot Examples (7 Travelers):

Budget-Friendly: SW morning flight + VRBO villa + 5-day base tickets + LL Multi Pass all days + 2 character meals + fireworks cruise + minivan = ~$10,000–$13,000

Mid-Range: SW morning flight + Moderate Resort (2 rooms) + 5-day Hopper + 1 VIP Tour day + Multi Pass other days + 3 character meals + cruise + minivan = ~$17,000–$23,000

Premium: SW flights + Deluxe Resort/Villa + 5-day Hopper + 2 VIP Tour days + character dining + fireworks cruise + Bibbidi Bobbidi + SUV = ~$25,000–$38,000+
All prices shown are starting estimates based on current published rates and our research. Final pricing confirmed at booking and may vary based on room selection, availability, date-based dynamic pricing, and applicable fees and taxes. Disney ticket prices, Lightning Lane pricing, and VIP Tour rates are subject to change without notice.
